fields.name: record_id
fields.description: Stable unique identifier for one MIC measurement record.

fields.name: peptide_sequence
fields.description: Amino acid sequence of the antibacterial peptide in single-letter code.
fields.normalization_rule: Uppercase; remove spaces and hyphens; keep standard one-letter amino acid codes only.

fields.name: peptide_length
fields.description: Number of amino acid residues in the peptide sequence. Derived from peptide_sequence.
fields.normalization_rule: Computed as len(peptide_sequence) after normalization. Used to filter outliers (typical range: 5–100 aa).

fields.name: molecular_weight_da
fields.description: Molecular weight of the peptide in Daltons. Required for converting MIC from µM to µg/mL.
fields.normalization_rule: Calculated from peptide_sequence using standard amino acid masses; adjust if modifications are present.

fields.name: peptide_name
fields.description: Common or reported name of the peptide, if available in the source.

fields.name: organism_source
fields.description: Biological origin of the peptide: species (binomial) or 'synthetic' / 'unknown'.
fields.normalization_rule: Use binomial species name for natural peptides; 'synthetic' for de-novo designed peptides.

fields.name: synthesis_type
fields.description: How the peptide was produced: ribosomal, nonribosomal, or synthetic.

fields.name: peptide_modifications
fields.description: Structured description of chemical or structural modifications: N-terminal, C-terminal, cyclization, D-amino acids, unusual residues, etc.

fields.name: pathogen_name
fields.description: Scientific name of the bacterial pathogen (species or genus).

fields.name: pathogen_strain
fields.description: Strain or isolate identifier when reported in the source.

fields.name: gram_stain
fields.description: Gram classification of the pathogen when known or reported.

fields.name: measurement_type
fields.description: Type of antimicrobial activity measurement; fixed to MIC for this dataset.

fields.name: measurement_value
fields.description: Numeric MIC value as reported in the source. Null if only a range or censored bound is given.

fields.name: normalized_value_ug_ml
fields.description: MIC converted to µg/mL for cross-record comparison.
fields.normalization_rule: Convert using original unit (from source text) and molecular_weight_da: µM × MW_Da / 1000 = µg/mL.

fields.name: assay_method
fields.description: Experimental method used to determine MIC.
fields.normalization_rule: Prefer standard terms: broth microdilution, agar dilution, agar diffusion, E-test.

fields.name: medium
fields.description: Base growth medium name when reported (MHB, LB, CAMHB, PBS, etc.).

fields.name: medium_composition
fields.description: Concentrations of substances in the assay medium.
fields.normalization_rule: Semicolon-separated substance concentrations as reported; do not infer unstated components.

fields.name: inoculum_cfu_ml
fields.description: Bacterial inoculum density used in the assay (CFU/mL). Affects absolute MIC values.
fields.normalization_rule: Store as reported string (e.g. '5×10^5', '1e6'). Standard CLSI inoculum is 5×10^5 CFU/mL.

fields.name: temperature_c
fields.description: Assay temperature in degrees Celsius.

fields.name: incubation_time_h
fields.description: Incubation time in hours during the MIC assay.

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: Identifier linking to an entry in specs/source_map.json.

fields.name: source_type
fields.description: Category of source (paper, database, web page, etc.).

fields.name: publication_year
fields.description: Year of publication or database entry. Useful for tracking data recency.

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: URL or landing page for the source.

fields.name: doi
fields.description: Digital Object Identifier when available.
